While some increase is linked to better #emergency care access, experts say commercialisation is also to blame https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: Payment systems often reward C-sections more than normal deliveries. In earlier Gujarat experiments, when private hospitals were paid a fixed amount r...
items.description_html: Payment systems often reward C-sections more than normal deliveries. In earlier Gujarat experiments, when private hospitals were paid a fixed amount regardless of delivery type, surgical rates stayed comparatively low, explains @DMavalankar: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: A normal delivery can take 6-12+ hours of monitoring. A C-section is scheduled and done in far less time. Experts point to this "arithmetic," plus the...
items.description_html: A normal delivery can take 6-12+ hours of monitoring. A C-section is scheduled and done in far less time. Experts point to this "arithmetic," plus the near-disappearance of trained midwives, as quiet drivers of rising surgery rates: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: A private hospital delivery costs Rs 39,000 on average—17 times more than in a public facility. In Tamil Nadu, private #childbirth costs nearly Rs 70...
items.description_html: A private hospital delivery costs Rs 39,000 on average—17 times more than in a public facility. In Tamil Nadu, private #childbirth costs nearly Rs 70,000, about 50 times the public-hospital cost. Commercialisation, rising #Csections are to blame: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: 40.5% of urban #births in India are by #Csection, compared to 22.8% in rural areas. Experts say private maternity care is increasingly the default eve...
items.description_html: 40.5% of urban #births in India are by #Csection, compared to 22.8% in rural areas. Experts say private maternity care is increasingly the default even outside big cities—reshaping how women experience childbirth: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: Jammu & Kashmir has the highest private-hospital C-section rate in India at 90%, followed by West Bengal (88%) and Telangana (84%). Nationally, privat...
items.description_html: Jammu & Kashmir has the highest private-hospital C-section rate in India at 90%, followed by West Bengal (88%) and Telangana (84%). Nationally, private hospitals deliver more than half of all births by #surgery: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: The @WHO says C-section rates above 10% don't improve maternal or #newborn survival at a population level. India's #private hospitals are at 54%. Publ...
items.description_html: The @WHO says C-section rates above 10% don't improve maternal or #newborn survival at a population level. India's #private hospitals are at 54%. Public hospitals, at 17%, are close to the "optimal" range. @PrachiSalve11 writes: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: Telangana has India's highest C-section rate (62.2%) and a maternal mortality ratio of 48 deaths per 100,000 live births. Tamil Nadu (47% C-section ra...
items.description_html: Telangana has India's highest C-section rate (62.2%) and a maternal mortality ratio of 48 deaths per 100,000 live births. Tamil Nadu (47% C-section rate) and Kerala (41%) report far lower MMRs—25 and 24. More surgery hasn't meant safer births: #Csection https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: Karnataka's C-section rate (46%) is nearly identical to Tamil Nadu's (47%). But its maternal mortality ratio—73 per 100,000 live births—is nearly th...
items.description_html: Karnataka's C-section rate (46%) is nearly identical to Tamil Nadu's (47%). But its maternal mortality ratio—73 per 100,000 live births—is nearly three times higher. Surgery rates alone don't explain outcomes, @PrachiSalve11 reports: https://www.indiaspend.com/health/why-c-section-deliveries-are-rising-in-india-987410

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: Happy to see my work in @thewire_in GST’s key feature: it’s self-enforcing. Firms need invoices to claim credits → they demand co...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
Happy to see my work in @thewire_in

GST’s key feature: it’s self-enforcing. Firms need invoices to claim credits → they demand compliance from suppliers → formalisation cascades through supply chains.

https://thewire.in/economy/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

from @Ideas4India

Paper
https://meghapatnaik.github.io/research/gst_prowess.pdf

Thrilled to share our paper on Sustainable Remote Workplaces (with @BeataJechorek , @asdurso & @ChloeThurstonDC ) is forthcoming in JSIS.

We show employees evaluate remote work as bundles of trade-offs: flexibility, pay, monitoring, & costs are considered jointly

#FutureOfWork

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit ...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
new blog post at @Ideas4India 45% of India’s manufacturing is informal. My work shows GST is slowly changing this: an input credit system creates a formalisation cascade where firms demand invoices from suppliers & this pulls the entire supply chain slowly into the formal economy


Ideas for India: Value-added tax has a self-enforcing design that generates formalisation cascades through supply chains. Using @_CMIE Prowess data on 12,024 firms for the period 2013-2022, Megha Patnaik @megonomista examines impact of India’s introduction of GST in 2017
https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/how-the-gst-created-a-formalisation-cascade

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: Global Capability Centres are projected to contribute to 3.5% of India's GDP. My new paper argues that GCCs are firm-level reorganis...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
Global Capability Centres are projected to contribute to 3.5% of India's GDP. My new paper argues that GCCs are firm-level reorganisation at the global scale: only the most productive firms set up GCCs, trading off lower wages against higher coordination costs of remote work.


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21294
Services Across Borders: How Firms Organize Remote Work at Scale
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/2gIf50YuL4m
#CEPR_ITRE #EconTwitter

items.title: RT Megha Patnaik: 45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enfo...
items.description_html:
RT Megha Patnaik
45% of India's GDP is informal. My new paper shows GST's credit mechanism is pulling firms into the formal economy, not through enforcement but because every buyer demands an invoice from the supplier behind it.
Formalisation cascading begins with large firms and grows upstream


CEPR: New CEPR Discussion Paper - DP21163
Self-Enforcing Tax Design and Supply Chain Formalization: Evidence from India’s GST Reform
Megha Patnaik @megonomista @UniLUISS
https://ow.ly/5QK550YfcL1
#CEPR_PE #EconTwitter

items.description_html:
RT Rahul Shukla
India's formal manufacturing productivity has more than doubled in 15 years. Real wages have barely moved since 2014. Just 13.7% of the value workers produce now reaches them, down from 22% two decades ago. I write for @Ideas4India at what is breaking the link.


Ideas for India: Despite ⬆️productivity in formal manufacturing in India, wages have been⬇️ or stagnant.

@rahulshuklamzp @The_IGC shows that in a given year, the more productive a state’s formal manufacturing sector, the smaller the share of that output flows to workers

https://www.ideasforindia.in/topics/macroeconomics/productivity-up-wages-flat-how-indias-manufacturing-growth-has-stopped-working-for-its-workers

items.description_html:
Axis capital on Coal India
ADD rating with TP at ₹485
🚨 E-auction volume +45% YoY in Q1FY27, realization also improves 🚨

1️⃣ Coal dispatch growth in June at 7.5% YoY is the highest in the last 28 months. Q1FY27 dispatch +3.5% YoY.

2️⃣ E-auction volume in Q1 +45% YoY aided by import substitution as Indonesian coal prices were high. E-auction premium over notified FSA price improved to 42% in June & 44% in Q1FY27 vs 36% in May'26 and 38% in FY26

3️⃣ Gains in e-auction earnings would be partly offset by cost inflation & wage hike provisions from Q2FY27. So they are trimming estimates by 3%-4%

items.title: Nuvama trims estimates on Jindal Steel: 👉 FY27/FY28 EBITDA cut by 9.5%/9.6% on lower volumes & higher cost of production/tn 👉 Maintains REDUCE w...
items.description_html:
Nuvama trims estimates on Jindal Steel:
👉 FY27/FY28 EBITDA cut by 9.5%/9.6% on lower volumes & higher cost of production/tn
👉 Maintains REDUCE with a target price of ₹1,018 vs ₹1,154

Key reasons:
1⃣ Long products (~48% of volumes) have seen a sharp price correction, while HRC prices are yet to adjust, pressuring Q2FY27 earnings.
2⃣ Higher costs are expected to weigh on Q1FY27.
FY27 volume estimates lowered due to weak H1 demand and a slower ramp-up of the 4.6 mtpa blast furnace.
3⃣ Meaningful cost savings from captive coal & pellet usage have yet to materialize.

items.title: This has interesting tax characteristics. If your employer pays higher than 1800 (to match your contribution, voluntarily) then they reduce your effec...
items.description_html: This has interesting tax characteristics. If your employer pays higher than 1800 (to match your contribution, voluntarily) then they reduce your effective salary to that extent, since CTC remains the same. This probably leads to lower tax outflow since employer contributions aren't taxable upto Rs. 7.5 lakh per year.

items.description_html:
𝗜𝘀 𝗜𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗮’𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝗻-𝗹𝗶𝗳𝗲 𝗶𝗻𝘀𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗴𝗿𝗼𝘄𝘁𝗵 𝗯𝗲𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗴𝗹𝘆 𝗿𝗲𝘁𝗮𝗶𝗹-𝗱𝗿𝗶𝘃𝗲𝗻?

CareEdge’s latest sector update points to a clear shift in industry dynamics, where growth is becoming more concentrated, and more competitive.

Key takeaways:

-Retail-led momentum strengthening: Industry premiums grew 8.7% y-o-y in May 2026, driven by health (↑13.7%) and motor segments, with retail health alone expanding 30.5% y-o-y.

-Private insurers & SAHIs gaining share: Private players grew 11.8%, while SAHIs surged 31.7%, pushing their combined market share to 70.9%—highlighting a decisive tilt toward customer-centric, retail-focused models.

-Segment divergence persists: Sharp contractions in fire (-24.5%) and crop insurance continue to offset broader gains, underscoring volatility in commercial and government-linked lines.

Read Report: https://www.careratings.com/uploads/newsfiles/1782196581_Health%20and%20Private%20Insurers%20Lead%20Non-Life%20Premium%20Growth%20in%20May%202026.pdf

items.description_html:
RT Barak Ravid
🇺🇸🇮🇷Witkoff and Kushner have been trying to convey to the Iranians that their demand for tolls could blow up a U.S.-Iran deal that would ultimately be far more lucrative for Iran.
🇺🇸🇮🇷"The U.S. message to Iran was 'Think bigger,'" a U.S. official said.
🇺🇸🇮🇷The official claimed the sums Iran could generate from developing and selling oil and other resources freely — if the U.S. lifted all sanctions under a deal — "would be 100 times more valuable to them than using a gangster tactic to try and charge a toll."

items.title: So far, it's a rush to export more and more oil. For sure, some barrels are coming from inventories. But the export surge is steadily supported by ris...
items.description_html:
So far, it's a rush to export more and more oil.

For sure, some barrels are coming from inventories. But the export surge is steadily supported by rising wellhead output.

Soon, it would be a battle for market share. At that point, we would know if it turns into a price war.

Bloomberg: Saudi Arabia’s crude oil exports have surged to roughly pre-war levels since the kingdom resumed cargo loadings inside the Persian Gulf https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-07-02/saudi-oil-exports-approach-pre-war-levels-as-hormuz-flows-rise?taid=6a46564d63792900018a292c&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_content=business&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter

items.description_html:
And Saudi Aramco is loading more tankers inside the Strait of Hormuz / Persian Gulf. Satellite imagery from earlier today shows 3 tankers taking crude in the Sea Islands at Ras Tanura and the SPMs at Al Juaima'h


Javier Blas: Four Saudi VLCCs have emerged this morning outside the Strait of Hormuz, with their draft indicating they are fully laden.

Three of them briefly turned their AIS on a few days ago in the area of Ras Tanura oil port, before going dark, while the fourth went dark earlier.

items.title: RT Alireza Talakoubnejad: Interesting excerpt from Ghalibaf's (Speaker of Parliament and head of the Islamic Republic's negotiating team) interview wi...
items.description_html:
RT Alireza Talakoubnejad
Interesting excerpt from Ghalibaf's (Speaker of Parliament and head of the Islamic Republic's negotiating team) interview with State TV in which he says that the Strait of Hormoz is valuable to Iran when there is *increasing* traffic in it.

He points out the Strait didn't have the geopolitical importance it did today 100 years ago when there was no oil industry moving through it (implying scaring away traffic will reduce Iran's leverage) and that it isn't in Iran's interest to make a situation where in the long run all countries come up with alternate routes.

As such it is very much in Iran's interest to make the Strait (and Iranian side of it) as appealing as possible & increase traffic.

This interview comes as there is an increase in vocal disagreement in Tehran between pro-deal factions led by Ghalibaf & Pezeshkian and opponents of the agreement.

items.title: RT Javier Blas: COLUMN: The hottest commodity? One that's suffering from a major supply shock. It may sound like I’m talking about oil, but this is e...
items.description_html:
RT Javier Blas
COLUMN: The hottest commodity?

One that's suffering from a major supply shock. It may sound like I’m talking about oil, but this is even more severe. Output is down 40% y-on-y and prices are up 80%.

The commodity in question? The anchovy.

items.title: In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY. Exports to China ...
items.description_html:

Exports to China fell for the 11th consecutive month, with China’s share of U.S. exports dropping to 3.8%. Exports to Taiwan and India rose YoY.

items.title: The US stock market has never been more dominant: US stock market capitalization is up to a record ~$81 trillion, now accounting for ~48% of global ma...
items.description_html:
The US stock market has never been more dominant:

US stock market capitalization is up to a record ~$81 trillion, now accounting for ~48% of global market cap.

This exceeds the world's 2nd largest stock market, China, at $17 trillion, by 375%.

The US stock market is now twice as large as that of China, Japan, Hong Kong, and Taiwan combined.

It is also larger than the next 18 stock markets combined.

The Magnificent 7 stocks alone are now larger than China's market value.

The current scale of the US stock market is unprecedented.

items.title: BREAKING: US technology funds attracted +$14.3 billion in inflows in the week ending July 1st, the 2nd biggest weekly inflow on record. This follows -...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: US technology funds attracted +$14.3 billion in inflows in the week ending July 1st, the 2nd biggest weekly inflow on record.

This follows -$9.3 billion in outflows the prior week and a record +$19.2 billion in inflows two weeks earlier.

This brings the 4-week average of weekly inflows to a record +$9.0 billion.

Tech funds are now on pace to attract +$152 billion in inflows in 2026, an all-time high.

Meanwhile, US equity funds recorded -$17.2 billion in outflows in the week ending July 1st, the largest weekly withdrawal since March.

Investors are aggressively rotating into technology.

items.title: Retail risk appetite is at record levels: 0DTE options contracts now account for a record 48% of total retail options volume. This percentage has more...
items.description_html:
Retail risk appetite is at record levels:

0DTE options contracts now account for a record 48% of total retail options volume.

This percentage has more than TRIPLED over the last 5 years.

Furthermore, 0DTE options reflected a record 30% of total US options volume in May.

By comparison, the 2022 high was ~18% while the average over the last 5 years is ~21%.

As a result, the average options contract now expires in under 3 days.

Retail demand for short-term options has never been higher.

items.title: Shocking stat of the day: The US labor force participation rate among 25 to 54-year-olds dropped -0.6 percentage points in June, the 2nd-largest month...
items.description_html:
Shocking stat of the day:

The US labor force participation rate among 25 to 54-year-olds dropped -0.6 percentage points in June, the 2nd-largest monthly decline on record in data going back to the 1940s.

This metric measures the proportion of Americans in prime age either working or actively looking for work.

The decline matches those seen in January 1968, March 1960, May 1953, October 1952, and June 1951.

The only larger monthly drop was recorded in April 2020, during the pandemic, when nearly the entire economy shut down.

As a result, the prime-age labor force participation rate fell to 83.3%, the lowest since December 2023.

Meanwhile, the total labor force participation rate has declined for 7 consecutive months, to 61.5%, the lowest since February 2021.

The US job market is rapidly deteriorating under the surface.

items.title: The AI spending boom is redefining the US economy: AI CapEx across Alphabet, $GOOGL, Amazon, $AMZN, Meta, $META, Microsoft, $MSFT, and Oracle, $ORCL, ...
items.description_html:
The AI spending boom is redefining the US economy:

AI CapEx across Alphabet, $GOOGL, Amazon, $AMZN, Meta, $META, Microsoft, $MSFT, and Oracle, $ORCL, is projected to rise to ~3.2% of US GDP in 2027.

If that materializes, annual CapEx will surpass national defense spending for the first time in history, which is projected at ~2.7% of GDP next year.

For this year alone, AI CapEx is set to increase to ~2.5% of GDP, from 1.5% in 2025, close to the estimated defense spending of ~2.7%.

This comes as AI CapEx of these 5 companies is projected to surpass $800 billion in 2026.

Subsequently, in 2027, this is expected to rise further, to a record $1.1 trillion.

Truly mind-blowing numbers.

items.title: The AI infrastructure buildout is entering a new phase: US tech companies are committing to spend a record $850 billion on data center leases over the...
items.description_html:
The AI infrastructure buildout is entering a new phase:

US tech companies are committing to spend a record $850 billion on data center leases over the next several years.

This marks a +$570 billion YoY increase, or +204%, and +$200 billion QoQ increase, or +31%.

Meta, $META, added the most in Q1 2026, committing +$79 billion in new leases, a +76% QoQ increase, bringing its total to ~$183 billion.

At the same time, Microsoft, $MSFT, added +$41 billion, a +26% QoQ increase, bringing its total to ~$197 billion.

Oracle leads with the largest total commitments at ~$250 billion, having already secured many of the key sites needed to fulfill its contract with OpenAI.

Tech companies are doubling down on AI.

items.title: BREAKING: The US goods trade deficit widened by -$22.8 billion, or -27%, in May, to -$105.8 billion, the largest since March 2025. This marks the larg...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: The US goods trade deficit widened by -$22.8 billion, or -27%, in May, to -$105.8 billion, the largest since March 2025.

This marks the largest monthly increase since November 2025.

US goods exports fell -$11.8 billion, or -5%, to $207.7 billion, the lowest since February, driven by a sharp decline in industrial supplies, particularly energy shipments.

Meanwhile, imports rose +$10.9 billion, or +4%, to $313.4 billion, the highest since March 2025.

The increase was driven by continued imports of data center equipment, including computers, semiconductors, and telecom equipment.

The trade deficit is widening again.

items.title: Leverage in South Korean chip stocks is out of control: Single-stock leveraged and inverse ETFs tracking SK Hynix now hold ~$19 billion in total asset...
items.description_html:
Leverage in South Korean chip stocks is out of control:

Single-stock leveraged and inverse ETFs tracking SK Hynix now hold ~$19 billion in total assets, more than 4 times the stock's average daily trading volume this year of ~$4.5 billion.

At the same time, Samsung has ~$12.4 billion in leveraged ETF assets, +176% above its ~$4.5 billion in average daily turnover.

Furthermore, the Hong Kong-listed 2x leveraged long SK Hynix ETF, which holds ~$13 billion in assets, is worth about twice the value of SK Hynix shares traded on an average day, the widest gap of any major stock with a leveraged ETF tracking it.

By comparison, Micron, $MU, has ~$9.9 billion in leveraged ETF assets, well below its ~$27.5 billion in average daily trading volume.

All while Tesla, $TSLA, and Nvidia, $NVDA, have leveraged ETF assets of ~$6.0 billion and ~$5.6 billion, both far smaller than their daily trading volumes of ~$23.6 billion and ~$28.8 billion, respectively.

Leverage concentration in Korean chip stocks is through the roof.

items.title: BREAKING: World central banks purchased +41 tonnes of gold in May, the largest monthly addition since November 2025. This follows +17 tonnes acquired ...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: World central banks purchased +41 tonnes of gold in May, the largest monthly addition since November 2025.

This follows +17 tonnes acquired in April, and marks the 3rd monthly purchase this year.

Poland led for the 2nd consecutive month at +18 tonnes, bringing its year-to-date total to +64 tonnes, with gold reserves now at a record 614 tonnes.

China added +10 tonnes, the biggest monthly addition since December 2024, increasing its official gold reserves to a record 2,331 tonnes, also accounting for 9% of total FX reserves, near an all-time high.

This also marks the 20th consecutive monthly purchase by the Chinese central bank.

At the same time, Uzbekistan and Kazakhstan acquired +9 tonnes and +7 tonnes, respectively.

Central bank demand for gold is back.

items.title: What is happening in Hong Kong? Chinese semiconductor exports to Hong Kong rose to a record $15 billion in May. This figure has soared over +200% sinc...
items.description_html:
What is happening in Hong Kong?

Chinese semiconductor exports to Hong Kong rose to a record $15 billion in May.

This figure has soared over +200% since early 2025.

At the same time, Chinese chip exports to the world surged to a record $35 billion, tripling over the same period.

As a result, Hong Kong accounted for 43% of China’s semiconductor exports in May.

Over the first 5 months of 2026, China exported $239 billion of chips globally, with Hong Kong absorbing over 50% of this total, a record proportion and up from ~33% a decade ago.

Hong Kong has become a dominant logistics hub for China’s chip trade.

items.title: Full-time jobs in the US are falling at a concerning rate: Full-time employment dropped -514,000 in June, to 133.66 million, the lowest since December...
items.description_html:
Full-time jobs in the US are falling at a concerning rate:

Full-time employment dropped -514,000 in June, to 133.66 million, the lowest since December 2024.

This marks the 3rd consecutive monthly decline, totaling -1.01 million.

Since January 2025, full-time employment has now fallen -2.24 million

This also brings full-time employment back to levels seen in Q1 2023.

Meanwhile, the full-time employment-to-population ratio, which measures the proportion of the population working full-time, is down to 48.5%, from 50.5% in 2022, the lowest since mid-2021.

The weakness in the US labor market is accelerating.

items.title: Japanese individual investors are buying the dip at a record pace: Retail investors purchased +$5.9 billion of Japanese equities in the week ended Jun...
items.description_html:
Japanese individual investors are buying the dip at a record pace:

Retail investors purchased +$5.9 billion of Japanese equities in the week ended June 26th, the largest weekly total on record.

At the same time, foreign funds sold -$7.7 billion, the largest weekly outflow since March.

This follows -$6.0 billion in sales by retail in the prior 2 weeks.

Stocks linked to AI and semiconductors were hit hardest last week, with SoftBank, Kioxia, and Furukawa Electric each falling at least -12% during the week.

Despite the pullback, the Nikkei 225 surged +37% in Q2 2026, its best quarterly performance on record, fueled by AI-related names.

Retail dip buying has become a global trend.

items.title: What is happening with the US construction sector? The hiring rate in US construction fell -0.3 percentage points in May, to 3.5%, the lowest since da...
items.description_html:
What is happening with the US construction sector?

The hiring rate in US construction fell -0.3 percentage points in May, to 3.5%, the lowest since data began in 2000.

Since January, this figure has declined -1.1 percentage points, extending its 4-year downtrend.

By comparison, the 2008 Financial Crisis and 2020 pandemic lows were 4.5% and 3.7%, respectively.

This comes as 295,000 new workers were hired in the construction sector in May, the 3rd-lowest monthly total since the 2020 pandemic.

Excluding 2020, this is the 3rd-lowest reading in 10 years.

US construction hiring is historically weak.

items.title: Companies that invest more in AI are also seeing greater workforce expansion: Firms with the highest AI spending posted a +10.2% increase in headcount...
items.description_html:
Companies that invest more in AI are also seeing greater workforce expansion:

Firms with the highest AI spending posted a +10.2% increase in headcount over the last 2 years, according to a study covering ~22,000 US companies.

At the same time, entry-level jobs rose +12.0% on average among these firms.

Job gains typically accelerate 6-12 months after a surge in AI spending.

By comparison, firms with low AI adoption saw headcount remain roughly unchanged over the same period.

In other words, companies that materially invest in AI tend to see stronger hiring, but only once investment reaches a level sufficient to unlock productivity gains.

AI is reshaping where hiring happens.

items.title: The AI Revolution is reshaping South Korea’s economy: The value of South Korea’s exports rose to a record ~$270 billion in Q2 2026. This comes as So...
items.description_html:
The AI Revolution is reshaping South Korea’s economy:

The value of South Korea’s exports rose to a record ~$270 billion in Q2 2026.

This comes as South Korea's exports jumped +59.5% YoY in June, an acceleration from the already strong +53.4% gain in May.

Semiconductor exports continue to lead the gains, surging +199.5% YoY, to $44.8 billion, driven by strong AI and data center investments.

Shipments of computer-related products soared +308.8%, while petroleum products rose +49.8%.

AI demand has made South Korea into an economic powerhouse.

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Brokerages on Grid Cos after sharp correction on Friday While Nomura Says - Overreaction to narrow exemption CLSA believes - Increased...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Brokerages on Grid Cos after sharp correction on Friday

While Nomura Says - Overreaction to narrow exemption
CLSA believes - Increased competition (ex-HVDC converters) is likely to weigh on pricing power & margins of domestic T&D equipment cos & Investors should be wary of lofty vals(PE of 50-90x)
@ShereenBhan
@_prashantnair @Reematendulkar
@CNBCTV18News @CNBCTV18Live
Detailed note 👇
Nomura on Industrials
Overreaction to narrow exemption
Order explicitly states it creates no precedent
Market players read it as a competitive threat & sold off listed power-equipment stocks such as - GE Vernova T&D India (Buy), CG Power (Buy), Hitachi
Energy Not rated) & Siemens Energy Not rated)
Exempted players won 9% of transmission-linked PGCIL tenders over FY09-20
Uneven capacity readiness across four entities: only one appears to be running near-optimal utilization
Exemption is explicitly sunset-bound, & grid infrastructure sits squarely in national-security bucket
Two-year window is too short to underwrite meaningful capacity expansion in India

Jefferies on Industrials
Media reports highlight that government has approved four Chinese companies (TBEA Energy, Nanjing Electric, New Northeast Electric, Taikai Electric) to supply equipment for government projects only from their Indian manufacturing facilities.
Import restrictions still remain.
Even with supply from these facilities, believe demand-supply mismatch remains.
Remain positive on Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy and see the correction as a buying opportunity.

Macquarie on Industrials
As per industry sources, government is set to exempt 4 Chinese players (detailed below), having manufacturing units in India, from the restriction imposed in February 2023 for critical power projects.
This exemption is valid for a period of two years (till June 2028)
Most of grid companies are in midst of capacity expansion and are likely to add capacity over the next 2-3 year, which is the validity of this exemption.
Should this exemption be to extend in future, long-term negative impact on margins of the incumbents, especially the 765kV GIS players cannot be ruled out
Barring TBEA, most of other companies have sub-scale operations in India & are likely to take time to ramp up
Believe this exemption is to address current shortage of certain of EHV grid equipment & unlikely to allow large scale Chinese imports in sensitive areas of grid infrastructure.

CLSA on Industrials
Indian government has granted a two-year exemption to four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers with manufacturing units in India, allowing them to participate in government/CPSE tenders.
More important is that this exemption was granted at request of Ministry of Power, validating case led by complaints from transmission utilities struggling with price inflation on controlled supply chains
Do not see this move impacting near-term volume growth, but increased competition (ex-HVDC converters) is likely to weigh on pricing power & margins of domestic T&D equipment cos such as Hitachi, GE Vernova T&D, BHEL (U-PF) & CG Power
Investors should be wary of lofty valuations (PE of 50-90x) on historic peak high margins led by ‘Make in India” driven shield but now govt seems to have taken cognisance of excessive margins being made by transformer sector.

items.title: META saying it is getting into the AI cloud business (to compete against Amazon's AWS & Microsoft's Azure) may be the most interesting new wrinkle in ...
items.description_html:
META saying it is getting into the AI cloud business (to compete against Amazon's AWS & Microsoft's Azure) may be the most interesting new wrinkle in the AI capex debate !

Here is why,

The bull case - is that this is not Meta backing down on AI spends. It is Meta trying to make shareholders more comfortable with massive capex by showing that unused or lower-priority compute can be monetised. In this reading, a potential 'MetaAWS' is a sign of a more sophisticated AI infra market - not the end of AI demand.

The bear case - If Meta is looking to rent out compute, is it because Zuckerberg has built ahead of actual internal demand ? That would make this an early warning signal for the AI capex chain - GPUs, memory, networking, packaging & semi equipment.

In a way the question isn't really whether Meta is spending too much money - it is - whether Meta is monetising abundance ? OR this is an admission of excess ?

For India, this debate matters as we all well know. India isn't part of the so called 'AI-trade'.. which has sucked in global equity flows for 2 years now. Consensus view is that as this trade begins to wobble, perhaps global capital would then start to look at markets like India ?

Weigh in with what you think.

#AI #Nifty #BankNifty #Technology #India #ITStocks

@CNBCTV18News

items.title: RT Maheshwer Peri: There’s a particular kind of fear that only a parent knows. The fear that comes watching your child choose a life you can’t quite...
items.description_html:
RT Maheshwer Peri
There’s a particular kind of fear that only a parent knows. The fear that comes watching your child choose a life you can’t quite protect them.

Vinayak is an engineer by education. He was in product, good at his job, well liked and adding value. Everything about it looked right from where I stood: Security, salary, work place etc.

But he felt that he didn’t fit. Not as a complaint but a quiet truth. He didn’t like the closed spaces, the stillness and the daily routine.

On a family holiday, he took to deep sea diving. He went back again, to the same diving site. He learnt. He trained. And he started interning with them and helping tourists. He would check the oxygen cylinders, give demos, instructions. And he soon got certification as a deep sea diving instructor.

And one fine day, he announced that he is quitting from his corporate life. He went abroad and learnt more. And more. And more. He trained. Got every certification - On diving, surviving etc.

He is now a deep sea diving instructor, working in Indonesia. He earls less than half the pay, carries cylinders, spends long hours underwater, lives without the comforts I spent a career trying to provide. No AC room. Not even a wash basin in his room. No safety net.

He is happy. Underwater, undercompensated, and, by every real measure, more himself than he ever was in that office.

My fear hasn’t fully gone. I don’t think it’s meant to. He didn’t need me to protect him from this choice. He needed me to trust that he knew himself better than I did. That’s the harder thing to learn, as a parent: sometimes love means stepping back and letting someone be honest, brave and trusted.

I am proud. He had the courage that I didn’t have. He gave up comforts and chose what his heart said. He trained. Learnt. He was happy to be a subordinate than be the promoters son. He chose the difficult path. The not so treaded path. He is happy. And I am proud.

Happy birthday Vinayak.

items.title: Chat with S. Naren of ICICI Pru AMC today. 5 points. Watch below. 1. "Clearly the macro is in much better shape.. but we still believe in moderate ret...
items.description_html:
Chat with S. Naren of ICICI Pru AMC today.

5 points. Watch below.

1. "Clearly the macro is in much better shape.. but we still believe in moderate returns.. still not in a period where you can give a thumping the table call on equity.. we have to still wait for the AI bucket to either start underperforming for India to get a big part of the FII flows"

2. "Midcaps are the most overvalued relative to large caps and small caps.. we are constructive on both large caps and on small caps.."

3. "You need the growth mindset and not defensive value mindset for you to play the 'electrification theme' (industrials/utlities etc)... these are not growing at 5-10% but much faster.. BUT if the global AI theme were to deflate, this would also deflate"

4. "Private sector banks have derated significantly that you should clearly expect decent returns from private sector banks now.."

5. "We are very positive on oil and gas sector”

#Nifty #BankNifty #Investing #Macros

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=W1RiA2ELYxo

India loves a narrative — until it forgets it. Railway stocks are down 50% from 2 years ago and nobody's talking about it anymore.

ABB went from 35x to 60x forward earnings — that's when we walked away

Cognizant at 7x, Accenture at 9x forward earnings — Kotak's Sanjeev Prasad says that's the US market pricing in a profit decline for IT services. His call: don't catch a falling knife.

Preferred themes: domestic services: financials, healthcare services, hospitality (travel, tourism, transportation), retailing and telecom, backed by a long domestic growth runway, large unorganized-to-organized market share shifts in three of the five sectors, and limited global competition in India

items.title: I finally have an announcement to make! I've been creating content for almost a decade, and so many of you have told me my work has impacted your inve...
items.description_html:
I finally have an announcement to make!

I've been creating content for almost a decade, and so many of you have told me my work has impacted your investing in some way. That means everything to me!

So a little over a year ago I decided to formalize things and got SEBI-registered. For a finance content creator already doing well, you might ask why add all that compliance and complexity. Simple, I felt quality stock market research that is also accountable was missing.

A lot of you in my circle have heard me mention an app I've been building. Since then I've built a team of around 20, mostly in research (fundamental and technical), plus tech, customer success, and operations.

Together, we built @RecoWealth, a platform and community for Quality Equity Research, Curated Stock Recommendations with Transparency, and a genuine community around all things investing in India and US.

We think most people are underexposed to equity in general, and even more so to global markets.

We didn't want to be just another place for stock tips so we've vertically integrated to be with you from the start of your investing journey and as a partner throughout.

Time to change the way people look at Research! We are coming soon with our App! Stay Tuned.....

items.title: One thing we always track is the promoter's stake! When promoters keep increasing their stake, it often signals confidence in the business. Paisalo Di...
items.description_html:
One thing we always track is the promoter's stake!

When promoters keep increasing their stake, it often signals confidence in the business.

Paisalo Digital is a good example. Promoter holding has steadily increased from ~26% in FY19 to 46.72% in Q1FY27.

Also, the company has guided for doubling its AUM, income, and PAT over the next 3 years, and the stock is up 20% today.

Disclosure: We have Paisalo Digital in our FLUID Q Smallcase basket.

items.description_html:
RT Timsy Jaipuria
Nine years after GST reshaped India's indirect tax landscape, gross GST collections for June 2026 came in at ₹1.95 lakh crore, up 13.9% YoY.

This 13.9% y-o-y increase, as per data, primarily looks to be driven by a sharp jump in import revenues.

Imports GST show a growth of about 34%, which experts say, could be, to some extent, on account of rupee depreciation.

Overall growth however depicts strong resilience by the Indian economy amidst geopolitical uncertainties

Govt data 👇

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: CLSA on Kotak Bk O-P, TP Rs 440 Announced acquisition of Deutsche Bank (DB) India’s retail banking, private banking and wealth manage...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
CLSA on Kotak Bk
O-P, TP Rs 440
Announced acquisition of Deutsche Bank (DB) India’s retail banking, private banking and wealth management business
This business comprised Rs290bn in loans, Rs160bn in deposits and Rs105bn in wealth AUM as of FY26
Purchase price for this business was Rs2.8bn plus the difference in assets and liabilities acquired as of the purchase date.
However, no detail about the profitability of the business was provided by co
Only comment made was it would be “ROE accretive”.

HSBC on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 460
KMB announced acquisition of Deutsche Bank’s India retail banking portfolio for a consideration of INR2.8bn
Portfolio adds c5.6% of loans, c2.8% of deposits, & c1.7% CASA to KMB’s existing balance sheet as of FY26
This acquisition does not meaningfully change outlook on stock

Nomura on Kotak BK
Buy, TP Rs 460
Kotak to acquire DBAG's retail, private banking & wealth business in India
Disciplined pricing: close to book value pricing reflects DBAG’s soft profitability profile
Good secured asset book, but a comparatively weaker liability franchise
Capital impact manageable; management guides to RoE accretion
View this is a sensible, disciplined acquisition, but don't see RoE/earnings benefit as substantial or immediate; success hinges on integration execution & client retention

Jefferies on Kotak Bk
Buy, TP Rs 450
Kotak Bank will acquire Deutsche Bank's India retail bkg, private bkg, & WM business for Rs2.8bn.
To Kotak's loans/deposits/AUM, it adds 6%/3%/1%; takes 84bps of CET1.
Deal looks accretive with scope to cross-sell; it comes days after Kotak's CEO chose not to renew his term after Dec-26
Conversation with Jefferies European Bk analyst indicates this trx may be aimed to simplify DB's structure & focus on core markets
@CNBCTV18Live @_RituSingh

items.title: The Chairman provides oversight. The CEO provides execution. The moment a Chairman starts running the bank, or a CEO starts dominating the board, you ...
items.description_html:
The Chairman provides oversight. The CEO provides execution.

The moment a Chairman starts running the bank, or a CEO starts dominating the board, you have a governance problem.

Checks and balances work only when both understand the very different parts they have to play.


R. Balakrishnan: Why do banks need to appoint a Chairman ? The CEO is where the buck stops. once a Chairman (part time or full time) collects wages, he becomes the defacto CEO and should be held accountable for all the good and the bad. What will he do sitting in the office?

items.title: Portfolio should have a mix of all themes in my opinion , few tilted to growth , value and optionality , allocation should be tilted to market dynamic...
items.description_html:
Portfolio should have a mix of all themes in my opinion , few tilted to growth , value and optionality , allocation should be tilted to market dynamics and margin of safety and where the opp size is very large

Getting too aggressive trying to be an extreme value/growth investor , unnecessarily putting yourself into buckets does not help in my opinion , have to find the best of both worlds tilted towards margin of safety and incremental growth and focus on management integrity and quality

People who only tilt to high growth may be stuck in sectors at the end of the cycle , and people who are too value oreiented might be too early to themes that even if stock does well, time based returns are not very impressive

Play as the pitch demands and opportunities present itself not according to biases or buckets

items.title: RT ValueEquity: I cannot emphasize this enough , many investors get lost in the depth of coverage universe and do not broaden it enough to capture gro...
items.description_html:
RT ValueEquity
I cannot emphasize this enough , many investors get lost in the depth of coverage universe and do not broaden it enough to capture growth ( it is not binary , both can be done)

Yes it is very important to have good sectoral insights and have a strong circle of competence , but always look to broaden what you read , new sectors , companies and more

The more you read , the more you reject , the more sectors you can cross analyse , the more companies you can actually understand , you are sharpening the edge

Depth + Breadth of coverage is a very long topic , but there is an optimum breadth-depth that we must develop , too much of any can be big opportunity cost !

items.title: Indo Borax: ₹20 Cr Boron Oxide Capex to Move Up the Value Chain Indo Borax is building its next phase of growth around 4 clear priorities — Boric Ac...
items.description_html:
Indo Borax: ₹20 Cr Boron Oxide Capex to Move Up the Value Chain

Indo Borax is building its next phase of growth around 4 clear priorities — Boric Acid leadership, Boron Oxide expansion, exports and R&D.

- Company will continue to strengthen its leadership in Boric Acid

- Focus remains on capacity enhancement and operational improvements

- Indo Borax is investing ~₹20 crore in a new Boron Oxide facility at Pithampur

- Planned Boron Oxide capacity: 4,000 MTPA

- This will help the company move into higher-margin Boron chemistry products

- International expansion is another key priority

- Focus markets include South America and South-East Asia

- R&D investments will be increased for specialised boron-based solutions

- Target applications include advanced materials, flame retardants and specialty agriculture

Key takeaway:

Indo Borax is trying to evolve from a Boric Acid-led company into a broader Boron chemistry platform.

The ₹20 crore Boron Oxide capex is small in size, but strategically important — it pushes the company towards higher-margin products and newer global markets.


Beat The Street: Indo Borax - Leader in Boric Acid

An import substitution play on new and emerging chemical products - Expanding Products and Integration

Going through a strategic transformation

items.title: Wrongful Removal of YouTube Channel @YouTubeIndia @YouTubeCreators @YTCreatorsIndia @TeamYouTube I am writing to formally appeal the recent notice sta...
items.description_html:
Wrongful Removal of YouTube Channel

@YouTubeIndia @YouTubeCreators @YTCreatorsIndia @TeamYouTube

I am writing to formally appeal the recent notice stating that my channel, Beat The Street, is under review for a violation of the "Harmful or Dangerous" content policy.

Currently, I have not received a notification pointing to any specific video, description, or timestamp that triggered this flag. Because of this, I am reaching out to request a manual human review of my channel to clear up what I believe is an automated misunderstanding.

Beat The Street is an educational channel dedicated to financial analysis, equity research, and corporate case studies. The content is strictly designed to educate viewers on capital markets and business strategies.

We absolutely do not depict, promote, or encourage any activities causing physical injury, death, or psychological harm. It is highly probable that YouTube’s automated system has misinterpreted standard financial terminology or metaphors used in our video titles and descriptions (such as "market bleeding," "corporate suicide," or "destroying the competition") and flagged them out of context.

I am fully committed to maintaining a safe community on YouTube. If a human reviewer determines that a specific piece of metadata or content is problematic, I am completely willing to edit or remove it immediately.

Could you please look into this case or clarify which specific content triggered the review so I can rectify it?

Thank you for your time, support, and guidance.

Sincerely,
Sudarshan Bhandari
Email ID: beetingdastreet@gmail.com

Channel Name: Beat The Street
Channel Link: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpCE9fZxdQy92Z5AWJ3zSaQ

items.title: Supriya Lifescience is down 10% today The fall is mainly due to a disclosure regarding judicial proceedings involving one of the company’s personnel ...
items.description_html:
Supriya Lifescience is down 10% today

The fall is mainly due to a disclosure regarding judicial proceedings involving one of the company’s personnel

Proceedings have been initiated against Mr. Sreekant Sreedharan, General Manager - Sales & Marketing, by the Special Investigation & Intelligence Branch of the Customs Department

The matter is under the NDPS Act, 1985 & relates to a procedural lapse in a single export transaction of an identified product

The company has clarified that, based on the information currently available, the proceedings are not expected to have any material impact on its operations

However, since the matter involves customs, market seems to be reacting cautiously

For now, this is not a business performance issue, but a governance / compliance related issue

items.title: In 2025, it corrected more than 50%. There were significant concerns & allegations surrounding the company back then Again in 2026, it corrected by al...
items.description_html:
In 2025, it corrected more than 50%.

There were significant concerns & allegations surrounding the company back then

Again in 2026, it corrected by almost 35%

This is how brutal volatility can be in equities particularly smallcaps

These sharp movements will keep happening

You name any stock, the journey will be full of volatility

If you want to make money in equities, you will have to make volatility your friend, not your enemy

End result is very rewarding, but corrections often throw investors out if they do not have the patience & conviction to hold through volatility

That is why the focus must be on the business rather than just the stock price

If the business is moving in the right direction, sooner or later the stock price usually follows the same trajectory

First initiated Yatharth when it was close to 350, have been riding it since then, while pyramiding along the way. Back then, the company operated 4 hospitals & now it has scaled to 9 hospitals. It has made a lot of progress over the years, guidance also looks interesting

Equity investors make a lot of money over the long term because the downside is limited, but the upside is limitless

That is the fun part of equity investing !


Equity Insights Elite: Yatharth ATH🔥

<450 : a brokerage stopped coverage, income tax raids, corporate governance concerns, expensive valuations…

Negativity sells fast

Now everything is fine!

items.title: Investing is difficult & tricky. Riding a stock is nothing short of a roller coaster Laurus Labs is a perfect example The stock went 10x in 2020-21 Th...
items.description_html:
Investing is difficult & tricky.

Riding a stock is nothing short of a roller coaster

Laurus Labs is a perfect example

The stock went 10x in 2020-21

Then it corrected more than 60% after touching close to 700

It bottomed around 280 in Apr’23

Back then, it was termed “Bogus Labs”🙃

Since then, the stock has moved more than 5x

Every stock goes through different cycles

If you catch the cycle right, you can make a lot of money

If you catch the cycle wrong, the same stock can test your patience badly

Usually, cycles last for around 2 years

This is why investing is not just about buying a good company

It is also about understanding cycles & having the patience to ride volatility


Equity Insights Elite: Laurus is not for everyone.

It's only for those who can handle its massive volatility and are prepared for temporary corrections.

This investment is only for individuals with a long-term horizon (FY 25,26)

Short-term movements can be erratic, but I've conducted my research,

items.title: RT Equity Insights Elite: Commentary across Jewellery Companies💎 Titan, Kalyan, P N Gadgil, Senco Gold, Thangamayil, D.P. Abhushan⏬ Organised jewe...
items.description_html:
RT Equity Insights Elite
Commentary across Jewellery Companies💎

Titan, Kalyan, P N Gadgil, Senco Gold, Thangamayil, D.P. Abhushan⏬

Organised jewellers delivered strong FY26, supported by higher gold prices, wedding demand, formalization & continued store expansion

Demand has remained resilient even at elevated gold prices

Jewellery buying in India is largely budget driven

Customers generally come with an INR budget rather than a volume budget

So when gold prices rise, grammage reduces, but ticket size & REV can still remain strong

This is why many organised jewellers have been able to report strong REV growth despite high gold prices

Another major trend is old gold exchange.

Old gold exchange is becoming a structural driver for the sector

It helps customers manage affordability, supports conversion & reduces dependence on fresh gold buying

For many jewellers, old gold exchange can move towards 50%+ share in FY27

At the same time, bars & coins saw strong demand in FY26 because of investment led buying during rising gold prices

But bars and coins are low margin products, so whenever the mix of bars & coins increases, margins get diluted

Going ahead, if coin & bar demand slows after the duty hike, customers shift more towards jewellery, it is positive for margins

To manage high gold prices, jewellers are focusing on affordability led products

This includes: Studded jewellery, Lightweight jewellery & Lower caratage jewellery

These products help customers stay within budget & also support margin improvement

Titan is also experimenting with newer formats like Hues & beYon, showing that large players are trying to create new category engines beyond traditional jewellery

Store expansion remains a key growth driver, especially for organised players entering newer geographies & capturing market share from smaller jewellers

Kalyan is expanding outside South India, PNG is scaling outside Maharashtra, Senco is also expanding beyond East India

Kalyan & PNG are scaling through FOCO led formats

This reduces capital intensity, improves ROCE & allows faster store expansion

Growth trajectory remains intact for FY27, with most organised jewellers guiding to maintain 20%+ growth, led by store expansion, SSSG & continued market share gains from unorganised players.

Harry Markowitz taught the world how to build optimal portfolios. For his own portfolio, he chose simplicity and behavioural understanding over mathematical optimization
What does that tell us about investing?
Explore this perspective in my commentary http://bit.ly/4vsCzBv

items.title: RT Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani: Today marks 250 years since a small group of newspaper editors, farmers, and soldiers signed a document declaring our n...
items.description_html:
RT Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani
Today marks 250 years since a small group of newspaper editors, farmers, and soldiers signed a document declaring our nation’s independence — a truth that feels self-evident now but was revolutionary then.

What a privilege us Americans have, to live in a nation that every one of its inhabitants can shape. What an honor us New Yorkers have, to look out over our city’s waters from the shores where so many Americans bravely entered their country for the first time.

Today and all days, let us remember that patriotism is not pretending our nation is without flaws. Patriotism is every act of righteous dissent — because loving our country means fighting for the best version of it.

Happy Fourth of July, New York City.

items.title: RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde: This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes w...
items.description_html:
RT Jesús Fernández-Villaverde
This is a photo I took of Keynes’s bedroom at Charleston House, Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant’s farmhouse. Keynes wrote The Economic Consequences of the Peace at this table, looking out over the pond (also notice Duncan Grant’s 1917 portrait of Keynes in the top left corner, painted in the garden).

Keynes liked the area so much that he later leased Tilton House, just down the lane.

As always, Keynes was a dominating figure. Rumor has it that he changed all the clocks in the house to his own eccentric time zone, “Charleston time,” which suited the household’s bohemian lifestyle. Whether this is true is hard to ascertain, but it fits the atmosphere quite well.

It was most interesting for me to be in the room where a book I teach every fall semester came to life. The curator, however, was intrigued by the fact that I seemed to care far more about Keynes than about Duncan Grant or Vanessa Bell. I apologized to her for my professional bias, pointing out that nobody is perfect!

items.title: RT Moses Harding John: Now that I am seeing the Life & General Insurance businesses closely, I notice that distributors walk away with big chunk of th...
items.description_html:
RT Moses Harding John
Now that I am seeing the Life & General Insurance businesses closely, I notice that distributors walk away with big chunk of the cake while the product manufacturers struggle to “contain” claim settlements (adjusted for reinsurance) & operating costs to stay in profit after adjusting the losses from core business against income from investment portfolio!

Cost of acquisition (distribution fees) + reinsurance + claim settlements + operating cost is always >100% net premium (gross premium adjusted for distribution fees + reinsurance) which is a funny business model! The profitability is high dependent on the performance of the investment portfolio, like an AMC!

Banks owning LI & GI (that includes health) businesses subsidize the cost of acquisition through their branch network to stay ahead in the race!

IRDAI needs to set up a fair play between the companies & distributors to make the insurance sector thrive & be customer efficient!

Coverage of risks around life & health and movable & immovable assets is as critical as banking & financial services sector, but unfortunately not provided the desired regulatory attention beyond governance & compliance!

Insurance sector is like a (life & non-life) risk-underwriting AMC!


Ira Dugal: The planned overhaul of insurance commission is set to be wide -- from staggered commissions over the lifecycle of the product to a dynamic pricing model based on effort involved in servicing the policy.

Much needed reforms with deep implications.

Reuters @AshwinM_ reports.

items.description_html:
US based Etched Semiconductor backed by Jane Street, HRT, Two Sigma, and Jump, 400+ engineers from NVIDIA, TPUs, Broadcom, SK Hynix, TSMC

$800M raised and $1B+ in contracts to take on Nvidia

Founder says that the engineers that finally helped then meet deadlines were in Bangalore, the team thats actually doing all the work!


Patrick OShaughnessy: Three years ago, two Harvard dropouts set out to build a better AI chip than the largest companies in the world.

items.description_html:
MCX HIT BY SHARP VOLUME DECLINE AS RBI BG RULE DISRUPTS TRADING

MCX recorded another sharp drop in trading volumes on Friday, with premium volumes falling further compared with both Thursday and Wednesday. Analysts attribute the weakness to the RBI’s new bank guarantee (BG) rules, whose impact appears significantly larger than initially expected. The effect is more pronounced on MCX because its clearing corporation, MCXCCL, relies heavily on bank guarantees for margin funding—around 60%—compared with peers such as BSE.

items.title: Another interesting point here is Peter Thiel's ability to read market cycles and knowing when not to invest. At the peak of the 2021 bubble, he wante...
items.description_html:
Another interesting point here is Peter Thiel's ability to read market cycles and knowing when not to invest.

At the peak of the 2021 bubble, he wanted the Founders Fund team to "go to the beach" for a year and stop investing.

But he knew the team would still want to deploy capital because the market was so hot.

So instead, he told them to identify the two or three best companies, invest at whatever the market-clearing price was, and ignore everything else.

One of those investments was Ramp, where Founders Fund invested at a $9 billion post-money valuation. Nine months later, Ramp had to raise a down round at $6 billion valuation.

But today, however, Ramp is worth around $40 billion - showing that backing good companies even if the timing is not ideal works out.

"I think there's something really unique about Peter. He understands that it's less important to be exactly right than to be directionally right, because it's really hard to shift the Overton window. Most people naturally moderate their views, and because of that they don't push hard enough to change strategy. Peter is different. He'll dig in his heels—not necessarily because he believes every word literally, but because he's trying to move the window, even if it's only a little. That small shift in thinking ends up being incredibly powerful," according to Trae Stephens of Founders Fund.


Madhav Chanchani: Partners from two top US venture firms, Benchmark and Founders Fund, say they are uncomfortable with the current funding frenzy because it reminds them of 2021.

“I’m very uncomfortable.....because it feels like we're getting back to a point where prices have become untethered

items.description_html:
One notable development for Peak XV's Surge portfolio has been the listing of Singapore-based Horizon Quantum Computing in the US through a SPAC few months ago.

Surge invested in Horizon's seed and Series A rounds in 2020 and 2022, respectively.

Today, Peak XV's stake in Horizon is worth more than $150 million.

items.title: The biggest shift in early-stage venture funding is that the bar for raising a Series A has become materially higher. "The benchmarks that you need to...
items.description_html:
The biggest shift in early-stage venture funding is that the bar for raising a Series A has become materially higher.

"The benchmarks that you need to hit to raise a Series A are just much higher," says Rajan Anandan of Peak XV Partners, adding that raising $1–2 million at the seed stage may no longer be enough to reach those milestones.

- AI startups may need several million dollars in ARR before attracting institutional Series A investors, while the cost of hiring top AI talent continues to rise.

- Consumer brands may need a clear path to a Rs 70–100 crore revenue run rate before they can attract meaningful follow-on capital.

This is one reason why Peak XV's seed-stage programme, Surge, has increased its maximum initial investment to $5 million, up from $1.5 million when it launched in 2019.

More here: https://www.thearcweb.com/article/peak-xv-surge-rajan-anandan-seed-iHmq9aXJYgEDrIP8

items.title: RT David Sacks: Legacy Media types are calling this Alex Karp interview a “crash-out” so that’s your first clue that he is actually saying somethin...
items.description_html:
RT David Sacks
Legacy Media types are calling this Alex Karp interview a “crash-out” so that’s your first clue that he is actually saying something extremely insightful. He is articulating what real “AI safety” looks like in the enterprise.

Not abstract alignment research or certification by a government-run DMV for AI. Real AI safety for businesses is the ability to control their own data, model weights, and compute — so a frontier lab can’t hoover up their proprietary knowledge and turn it into their next product.

As Karp explains, technical customers want “control over their compute, their models, their data stack, and their alpha. They want to know they own the means of production, and it’s not being transferred to someone else.”

Don’t think that can happen? Just look at Figma. According to The Information, Anthropic “blindsided” its then-business partner with the launch of Claude Design. Figma’s founder said Anthropic had not been “consistently honest” with them. Anthropic’s chief product officer had even served on Figma’s board until three days before the launch of Claude Design. Figma’s stock has fallen sharply this year while Anthropic’s valuation has surged.

This isn’t an isolated example. Anthropic has launched Claude Science, Claude Security, Claude Legal, and of course Claude Code — each expanding into categories previously served by companies building on top of their models. The pattern is consistent: watch where value is being created, then move in directly. Dominate the model layer, then use that position to capture the most lucrative verticals.

Dario has argued that open source models powerful enough to compete with Anthropic are “dangerous.” But dangerous to whom? Not to enterprises that want to retain control over their data and workflows. Dangerous to a business model that benefits from customers having few real alternatives at the model layer.

As Karp exposes, true enterprise safety isn’t trusting that a lab’s future roadmap won’t include your business. It’s retaining the ability to choose — at the model layer — who gets to see and use your alpha.

“What the technical customers want is control over their compute, their models, their data stack, and their alpha. They want to know they own the

items.title: RT Bull Theory: BREAKING: OpenAI has reportedly proposed giving a 5% equity stake to the U.S. government, according to the Financial Times. At OpenAI'...
items.description_html:
RT Bull Theory
BREAKING: OpenAI has reportedly proposed giving a 5% equity stake to the U.S. government, according to the Financial Times.

At OpenAI's current $852 billion valuation, a 5% stake would be worth $42.6 billion.

CEO Sam Altman has reportedly pitched the idea directly to President Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as a way to align the public with AI's economic upside and reduce political resistance.

The proposal would encourage other leading U.S. AI companies - including Anthropic, Google and Meta to contribute similar stakes to a sovereign wealth-style fund modeled after the Alaska Permanent Fund, which pays dividends from state investment returns.

The discussions are preliminary and any arrangement would likely require approval from Congress.

items.title: RT Jawwwn: Palantir CEO Alex Karp: "When OpenAI or Anthropic especially are talking about critical infrastructure—it only is being used in our produc...
items.description_html:
RT Jawwwn
Palantir CEO Alex Karp:

"When OpenAI or Anthropic especially are talking about critical infrastructure—it only is being used in our product. My product."

"Just so you see I'm not throwing shade—Dario is a literally historic figure."

"He came from behind, and he's now number one."

"I've been in business for a long time. Never seen anything like this."

"The reality is—critical infrastructure does not run these models without an application layer. In almost all cases, and maybe it won't be all cases someday, that application layer is our ontology. That's a fact."

Via @CNBC


Jawwwn: Palantir CEO Alex Karp on Anthropic's Dario Amodei:

"Most of the things they talk about in public are running on Palantir."

"He's gone from being way behind, to being ahead."

"I'll tell you—he believes what he's saying."

Via @CNBC

items.title: Hero MotoCorp’s bet on Ather Energy may be one of the best examples of a corporate investment in an Indian startup. Hero has already made nearly 8x o...
items.description_html:
Hero MotoCorp’s bet on Ather Energy may be one of the best examples of a corporate investment in an Indian startup.

Hero has already made nearly 8x on its cumulative investment of about Rs 1,700 crore.

Its 30% stake is now worth roughly Rs 13,000 crore.

Ather Energy's market capitalisation touched a new high of Rs 44,000 crore today, nearly four times its valuation at last year's IPO.

Back in 2017, Hero first invested Rs 200 crore for a 28% stake in Ather, then a four-year-old startup valued at just Rs 635 crore at that time.

Hero continued backing the company through multiple funding rounds and the IPO, and is now a co-promoter alongside founders Tarun Mehta and Swapnil Jain - who together own about a 10% stake.

OpenAI engineers earlier this month developed an optimization that cut inference costs in half for models it was applied to.

After the optimization was applied to logged-out ChatGPT traffic, it reduced the number of GPUs needed to power that traffic to a couple hundred.
